claim: The repository has a broad public website surface for review.
boundary: Website presence is not empirical AGI/ASI/SOTA proof.

claim: The repository has local implementation modules behind the website story.
boundary: Reference implementation is public-alpha, not production certification.

claim: The repository has evidence and report artifacts.
boundary: Generated artifacts are not independent external validation.

claim: The public boundary says no user data, no user funds, no wallet, no transaction, and human review required.
boundary: Boundary text is not jurisdiction-specific legal advice.

claim: The public demos avoid common browser network/storage/wallet APIs.
boundary: Static scan is not formal security certification.
Passing tests are not production certification.
